1950.event: India becomes a Republic — the Constitution takes effect on January 26
1950.fun_fact: India's first general election was being planned, the largest democratic exercise in history

1955.event: First Five-Year Plan completed — focus on agriculture and dams
1955.fun_fact: The Hindu Marriage Act was passed, reforming marriage laws

1960.event: Bombay state split into Maharashtra and Gujarat
1960.fun_fact: India's population crossed 400 million, making it one of the most populous nations

1965.event: Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 — ceasefire after 17 days
1965.fun_fact: The Green Revolution was about to transform Indian agriculture

1970.event: Indira Gandhi consolidates power — 'Garibi Hatao' campaign begins
1970.fun_fact: India was preparing for the Bangladesh Liberation War (1971)

1975.event: The Emergency declared — civil liberties suspended for 21 months
1975.fun_fact: India launched its first satellite, Aryabhata, into space

1980.event: Indira Gandhi returns to power after the Janata Party era
1980.fun_fact: India's first color TV broadcast was just around the corner (1982)

1985.event: Rajiv Gandhi modernizes India — computerization push begins
1985.fun_fact: The Bhopal gas tragedy had just occurred (1984), the world's worst industrial disaster

1990.event: Mandal Commission recommendations implemented — massive protests
1990.fun_fact: India was on the brink of an economic crisis that would lead to 1991 liberalization

1995.event: India's economy booming post-1991 liberalization — IT sector rising
1995.fun_fact: VSNL launched India's first public internet service on August 15

2000.event: India crosses 1 billion population — major IT boom underway
2000.fun_fact: Infosys, Wipro, and TCS were becoming global tech giants

2005.event: Right to Information Act passed — landmark transparency law
2005.fun_fact: India's economy was growing at 9%+ annually

2010.event: Commonwealth Games held in Delhi — India on the world stage
2010.fun_fact: Aadhaar (unique ID system) was launched, the world's largest biometric database

2015.event: Digital India campaign launched — pushing technology to rural areas
2015.fun_fact: Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was successfully orbiting Mars, making India the first Asian nation to do so

2020.event: COVID-19 lockdown — 1.3 billion people under the world's largest lockdown
2020.fun_fact: India produced the world's largest number of COVID vaccines (Covishield & Covaxin)

2025.event: India becomes the world's most populous nation — AI & space ambitions growing
2025.fun_fact: ISRO's Chandrayaan-3 had successfully landed on the Moon's south pole (2023)

1950.event: Korean War begins — US sends troops to the Korean Peninsula
1950.fun_fact: The average American family earned about $3,300 per year

1955.event: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her seat — Civil Rights movement ignites

1960.event: JFK elected as the youngest President — 'New Frontier' begins
1960.fun_fact: The first televised presidential debates changed politics forever

1965.event: Voting Rights Act signed — landmark civil rights legislation
1965.fun_fact: The United States had just entered the Vietnam War in earnest

1970.event: Kent State shootings — anti-war protests reach a peak
1970.fun_fact: The first Earth Day was celebrated on April 22

1975.event: Fall of Saigon — the Vietnam War officially ends
1975.fun_fact: Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen

1980.event: Iran Hostage Crisis dominates the news — Ronald Reagan elected
1980.fun_fact: Pac-Man was released and became an instant cultural icon

1985.event: Live Aid concert raises millions — 'We Are the World' era
1985.fun_fact: The NES (Nintendo) launched in the US, starting the gaming revolution

1990.event: Gulf War begins — Operation Desert Shield deployed
1990.fun_fact: The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit

1995.event: Oklahoma City bombing — deadliest domestic terrorism attack at the time
1995.fun_fact: Windows 95 launched to massive fanfare — people camped outside stores

2000.event: Y2K passes without incident — Bush vs Gore recount drama
2000.fun_fact: The dot-com bubble was at its peak before crashing

2005.event: Hurricane Katrina devastates the Gulf Coast
2005.fun_fact: YouTube was founded — the age of online video was born

2010.event: Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) signed into law
2010.fun_fact: Instagram was launched and gained 1 million users in 2 months

2015.event: Same-sex marriage legalized nationwide by the Supreme Court
2015.fun_fact: SpaceX successfully landed a Falcon 9 rocket for the first time

2020.event: COVID-19 pandemic & George Floyd protests reshape America
2020.fun_fact: SpaceX Crew Dragon became the first private spacecraft to carry astronauts to the ISS

2025.event: AI revolution transforms workplaces — ChatGPT and AI agents go mainstream
2025.fun_fact: Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon this decade

1950.event: NHS is 2 years old and transforming British healthcare
1950.fun_fact: Britain was still recovering from WWII with food rationing in place

1955.event: Winston Churchill retires — Eden takes over as PM
1955.fun_fact: ITV launched, breaking the BBC's TV monopoly

1960.event: 'Wind of Change' speech — decolonization of Africa accelerates
1960.fun_fact: The Beatles were forming in Liverpool

1965.event: The Beatles receive MBEs from the Queen
1965.fun_fact: The death penalty was abolished for murder in Great Britain

1970.event: UK struggles with economic challenges — 'three-day week' era approaching
1970.fun_fact: The first Glastonbury Festival was held (1970)

1975.event: UK votes to stay in the European Economic Community (EEC)
1975.fun_fact: Monty Python and the Holy Grail was released

1980.event: Thatcher's economic reforms reshape Britain — 'There is no alternative'
1980.fun_fact: John Lennon was assassinated in New York

1985.event: Live Aid concert at Wembley raises £150M for Ethiopian famine relief
1985.fun_fact: The miners' strike had just ended after a year-long battle

1990.event: Thatcher resigns — John Major becomes PM. Poll Tax riots
1990.fun_fact: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN

1995.event: Channel Tunnel opens — Britain is physically linked to continental Europe
1995.fun_fact: Britpop was at its peak — Oasis vs Blur defined the era

2000.event: Millennium Dome opens — London celebrates the year 2000
2000.fun_fact: The UK had the fastest growing economy in the G7

2005.event: London wins bid for 2012 Olympics — 7/7 bombings shock London the next day
2005.fun_fact: The UK signed the Kyoto Protocol on climate change

2010.event: Coalition government formed — first in decades
2010.fun_fact: The wedding of Prince William and Kate Middleton was approaching (2011)

2015.event: Brexit referendum looming — EU debate dominates politics
2015.fun_fact: Adele's '25' became the fastest-selling album in UK history

2020.event: Brexit officially happens — UK leaves the EU on January 31
2020.fun_fact: Captain Tom Moore raised £33M for the NHS by walking laps in his garden

2025.event: Labour government focuses on economic recovery and green energy
2025.fun_fact: King Charles III continues the new era of the British monarchy

1950.fun_fact: Canada sent troops to the Korean War as part of the UN force

1955.fun_fact: The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation (CBC) was expanding TV coverage

1960.event: Canadian Bill of Rights passed — first federal human rights law
1960.fun_fact: The Quiet Revolution was beginning in Quebec

1965.event: Canada adopts the Maple Leaf flag — replacing the Red Ensign
1965.fun_fact: Universal healthcare (Medicare) was being rolled out across provinces

1970.event: October Crisis — FLQ kidnappings lead to the War Measures Act
1970.fun_fact: Canada was hosting Expo 67's legacy and preparing for the 1976 Olympics

1975.event: CN Tower construction completed — tallest free-standing structure in the world
1975.fun_fact: Oil prices were reshaping Alberta's economy

1980.fun_fact: Terry Fox began his Marathon of Hope across Canada

1985.event: Free trade negotiations with the US begin
1985.fun_fact: Wayne Gretzky was dominating the NHL with the Edmonton Oilers

1990.event: Oka Crisis — 78-day standoff over Indigenous land rights
1990.fun_fact: The Meech Lake Accord failed, reigniting Quebec separatism

1995.event: Second Quebec referendum — 'Non' wins by just 1.2%
1995.fun_fact: Canada was becoming a tech hub with companies like Nortel and BlackBerry

2000.event: Nunavut territory is 1 year old — Canada's newest territory
2000.fun_fact: Canada had the fastest-growing G7 economy

2005.event: Same-sex marriage legalized nationally — fourth country in the world
2005.fun_fact: BlackBerry was the hottest tech company in North America

2010.event: Vancouver Winter Olympics — 'Own the Podium' campaign succeeds
2010.fun_fact: Sidney Crosby's golden goal in OT became Canada's greatest hockey moment

2015.event: Justin Trudeau elected — promises 'sunny ways' and reconciliation
2015.fun_fact: Canada welcomed 25,000 Syrian refugees over the winter

2020.event: COVID-19 shuts down Canada — CERB payments support millions
2020.fun_fact: The US-Canada border was closed to non-essential travel for the first time in history

2025.event: Canada pushes AI regulation and clean energy investment
2025.fun_fact: Canada's population grew rapidly due to record immigration levels

1950.event: Post-war migration boom — 'Populate or Perish' policy
1950.fun_fact: The Snowy Mountains Hydroelectric Scheme was underway

1955.event: Television arrives in Australia ahead of the 1956 Melbourne Olympics
1955.fun_fact: Rock 'n' roll was sweeping Australian youth culture

1960.event: Australia sends military advisors to Vietnam
1960.fun_fact: The Sydney Opera House was under construction

1965.event: Australian troops deployed to Vietnam — controversy grows
1965.fun_fact: Decimal currency (dollars and cents) was about to replace pounds and shillings

1970.event: Anti-Vietnam War moratorium marches draw hundreds of thousands
1970.fun_fact: Sydney Opera House was nearing completion

1975.event: The Dismissal — Governor-General fires PM Whitlam in constitutional crisis
1975.fun_fact: Colour television was introduced to Australia

1980.fun_fact: AC/DC released 'Back in Black', one of the best-selling albums ever

1985.event: Australia wins the America's Cup — massive national celebration
1985.fun_fact: Crocodile Dundee was about to make Paul Hogan an international star

1990.event: Economic recession hits — 'the recession we had to have'
1990.fun_fact: The World Wide Web was being invented, and Australia would be an early adopter

1995.event: Mabo decision reshapes Indigenous land rights in Australia
1995.fun_fact: Australia was preparing to host the 2000 Sydney Olympics

2000.event: Sydney Olympics — the 'best Olympics ever' according to IOC
2000.fun_fact: Cathy Freeman's gold medal run united the nation

2005.fun_fact: Steve Irwin was Australia's most famous person internationally

2010.event: Julia Gillard becomes Australia's first female PM
2010.fun_fact: Mining boom was driving the economy — Australia avoided the Global Financial Crisis

2015.event: Marriage equality debate intensifies across the country
2015.fun_fact: Australian housing prices in Sydney and Melbourne were among the world's highest

2020.event: Black Summer bushfires devastate eastern Australia — then COVID-19 hits
2020.fun_fact: Australia had some of the strictest COVID lockdowns in the world

2025.event: Australia invests heavily in renewable energy and AUKUS security pact
2025.fun_fact: Australia is one of the world's largest exporters of lithium for EV batteries

1950.event: Japan under post-war reconstruction — Korean War boosts Japanese industry
1950.fun_fact: Japan was still under significant US occupation influence

1955.fun_fact: Sony had just released its first transistor radio

1960.event: Income Doubling Plan launched — Japan aims to double GDP in 10 years
1960.fun_fact: Japan was preparing to host the 1964 Tokyo Olympics

1965.event: Shinkansen (bullet train) is 1 year old — revolutionizing transport
1965.fun_fact: Japan's GDP had grown to become the world's second-largest economy

1970.event: Osaka Expo 70 — 64 million visitors experience Japan's future vision
1970.fun_fact: Japan had the world's fastest trains and was a tech manufacturing powerhouse

1975.event: Japan rebounds from oil crisis — becomes world leader in fuel-efficient cars
1975.fun_fact: Space Invaders was being developed (released 1978), launching the arcade era

1980.event: Japan becomes the world's largest car manufacturer
1980.fun_fact: The Walkman (released 1979) was transforming how the world listens to music

1985.event: Plaza Accord signed — yen strengthens, reshaping Japan's economy
1985.fun_fact: Super Mario Bros. was released, becoming the best-selling game of all time

1990.event: Japanese asset bubble bursts — beginning of the 'Lost Decade'
1990.fun_fact: The Nikkei stock index had peaked at 38,957 on December 29, 1989

1995.event: Great Hanshin earthquake kills 6,434 — Aum Shinrikyo sarin attack in Tokyo subway
1995.fun_fact: Sony PlayStation was revolutionizing gaming worldwide

2000.event: Japan enters the new millennium — still in economic stagnation
2000.fun_fact: Japanese anime and manga were becoming global cultural phenomena

2005.event: Japan's population peaks — demographic decline begins
2005.fun_fact: Nintendo DS and Wii were about to make Nintendo culturally relevant again

2010.event: China surpasses Japan as world's 2nd largest economy
2010.fun_fact: Japan's Hayabusa spacecraft returned asteroid samples to Earth — a first

2015.event: 'Abenomics' reforms attempt to revive the economy
2015.fun_fact: Japan was preparing for the 2020 Tokyo Olympics

2020.event: Tokyo Olympics postponed due to COVID-19
2020.fun_fact: Japan's population had been declining for over a decade

2025.event: Japan embraces AI and robotics to address population decline
2025.fun_fact: Japan leads the world in humanoid robotics and elderly care technology

1950.fun_fact: Germany was still divided into East and West

1955.event: West Germany joins NATO — regains sovereignty
1955.fun_fact: Volkswagen Beetle was becoming the world's most popular car

1960.event: Berlin Wall is about to be built (1961) — Cold War tensions at peak
1960.fun_fact: West Germany had one of the fastest economic growths in the world

1965.event: Auschwitz trials conclude — Germany confronts its past
1965.fun_fact: Guest worker (Gastarbeiter) program was bringing millions of workers

1970.event: Brandt's Ostpolitik — historic kneel at the Warsaw Ghetto memorial
1970.fun_fact: West Germany won the 1974 World Cup, hosted on home soil

1975.event: Germany deals with Red Army Faction terrorism
1975.fun_fact: BMW, Mercedes-Benz, and Volkswagen were becoming global automotive leaders

1980.event: Peace movement grows — protests against nuclear weapons
1980.fun_fact: Germany was the economic powerhouse of Europe

1985.event: Cold War tensions high — but change is coming
1985.fun_fact: Boris Becker became the youngest Wimbledon champion at age 17

1990.event: German reunification — the Berlin Wall had fallen on November 9, 1989
1990.fun_fact: Germany would go on to host and win the World Cup in 2006 and 2014

1995.event: Reunified Germany navigates economic challenges of integration
1995.fun_fact: SAP was becoming one of the world's largest software companies

2000.event: Germany adopts the Euro — replacing the Deutsche Mark
2000.fun_fact: Germany was one of the founding members of the Eurozone

2005.event: Angela Merkel becomes Germany's first female Chancellor
2005.fun_fact: Germany was preparing to host the 2006 FIFA World Cup

2010.event: Germany leads European response to the sovereign debt crisis
2010.fun_fact: Germany's renewable energy transition (Energiewende) was accelerating

2015.event: Merkel opens borders to refugees — 'Wir schaffen das' (We can do this)
2015.fun_fact: Over 1 million refugees arrived in Germany that year

2020.event: COVID-19 response — Germany's handling praised early in pandemic
2020.fun_fact: BioNTech co-developed the first authorized mRNA COVID vaccine

2025.event: Germany accelerates green energy and digital transformation
2025.fun_fact: Germany remains Europe's largest economy and industrial powerhouse

1950.event: Brazil hosts and loses the 1950 FIFA World Cup final to Uruguay (Maracanazo)
1950.fun_fact: The Maracanã stadium held nearly 200,000 people for the final

1955.event: Juscelino Kubitschek elected — plans to build Brasília
1955.fun_fact: Bossa nova music was emerging in Rio de Janeiro

1960.event: Brasília inaugurated as the new capital — a modernist marvel
1960.fun_fact: Pelé was already a World Cup champion at age 17 (1958)

1965.event: Military dictatorship consolidates — political repression grows
1965.fun_fact: Brazilian music was going global with bossa nova and Tropicália

1970.event: Brazil wins the 1970 World Cup — considered the greatest team ever

1975.event: Brazilian Miracle economy — rapid growth but inequality persists
1975.fun_fact: Brazil was one of the world's largest coffee exporters

1980.event: Abertura — slow transition back to democracy begins
1980.fun_fact: Carnival in Rio was becoming one of the world's most famous festivals

1985.event: Military rule ends — civilian government returns to Brazil
1985.fun_fact: Brazil's external debt crisis was reshaping the economy

1990.event: Collor Plan freezes bank accounts to fight hyperinflation
1990.fun_fact: Brazilian hyperinflation reached over 1,000% per year

1995.event: Plano Real stabilizes the economy — inflation finally tamed
1995.fun_fact: Brazil won the 1994 World Cup in the USA

2000.event: Brazil celebrates 500 years since Portuguese arrival
2000.fun_fact: Brazilian telenovelas were watched across Latin America and beyond

2005.event: Bolsa Família program lifts millions out of poverty
2005.fun_fact: Brazil was selected to host the 2014 FIFA World Cup

2010.event: Brazil's economy booms — rises to 7th largest in the world
2010.fun_fact: Brazil was also awarded the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio

2015.event: Economic crisis and political scandal — impeachment proceedings begin
2015.fun_fact: Zika virus outbreak becomes a major health concern

2020.event: COVID-19 hits Brazil hard — Amazon deforestation concerns grow
2020.fun_fact: Brazil had the 2nd highest COVID case count globally

2025.event: Lula returns — focuses on Amazon protection and social programs
2025.fun_fact: Brazil remains the world's largest coffee producer and a biodiversity superpower

1950.event: France rebuilds under the Fourth Republic — Schuman Declaration founds the EU
1950.fun_fact: French cinema was entering its golden age

1955.event: Algerian War intensifies — decolonization crisis grows
1955.fun_fact: Citroën DS was unveiled, stunning the automotive world

1960.event: France tests its first nuclear weapon — becomes a nuclear power
1960.fun_fact: French New Wave cinema was revolutionizing filmmaking worldwide

1965.event: De Gaulle re-elected — France pursues independent foreign policy
1965.fun_fact: The Concorde supersonic jet was being developed with Britain

1970.event: De Gaulle dies — end of an era for France

1975.event: France modernizes — voting age lowered to 18

1980.event: François Mitterrand about to become president (1981) — leftist turn
1980.fun_fact: France was the world's top tourist destination

1985.event: Rainbow Warrior bombing — French agents sink Greenpeace ship
1985.fun_fact: The Louvre Pyramid was being designed by I.M. Pei

1990.event: German reunification reshapes European politics — France adapts
1990.fun_fact: France was preparing for the Maastricht Treaty and European Union formation

1995.event: Chirac elected president — nuclear tests in the Pacific spark protests

2000.event: France wins Euro 2000 — golden era of French football

2005.event: French voters reject EU Constitution in referendum

2010.event: Pension reform protests — massive strikes across France
2010.fun_fact: France remained the world's most visited country

2015.event: Charlie Hebdo and November 13 Paris attacks — 'Je suis Charlie'
2015.fun_fact: Paris hosted COP21 — the Paris Climate Agreement was signed

2020.event: COVID-19 lockdowns — France implements strict containment measures
2020.fun_fact: Notre-Dame was still being rebuilt after the 2019 fire

2025.event: France pushes European strategic autonomy and AI investment
2025.fun_fact: The restored Notre-Dame Cathedral reopened in December 2024

1955.event: Post-war reconstruction — South Korea is one of the poorest countries
1955.fun_fact: South Korea's GDP per capita was lower than many African nations

1960.event: April Revolution topples Syngman Rhee — student protests succeed
1960.fun_fact: South Korea was still largely agricultural with limited industry

1965.event: Normalization of relations with Japan — controversial but economically vital
1965.fun_fact: The 'Miracle on the Han River' economic transformation was beginning

1970.event: Rapid industrialization — Hyundai, Samsung, LG emerging as conglomerates

1975.event: Heavy and Chemical Industries drive — Korea becomes an industrial power

1980.event: Gwangju Uprising — pro-democracy movement violently suppressed

1985.event: Democratization movement grows — 1987 June Struggle approaching

1990.event: South Korea establishes diplomatic relations with the Soviet Union

1995.event: South Korea joins OECD — recognized as a developed economy
1995.fun_fact: Samsung and LG were becoming major global electronics brands

2000.event: Historic inter-Korean summit — Kim Dae-jung wins Nobel Peace Prize

2005.event: Hallyu (Korean Wave) begins spreading globally — K-dramas and K-pop
2005.fun_fact: South Korea had the world's fastest internet speeds

2010.event: G20 Summit hosted in Seoul — Korea on the world stage
2010.fun_fact: K-pop was going viral globally, with PSY's Gangnam Style coming in 2012

2015.event: MERS outbreak tests Korea's healthcare system
2015.fun_fact: BTS debuted in 2013 and was about to become the biggest boy band in the world

2020.event: Parasite wins Academy Award for Best Picture — first non-English film
2020.fun_fact: South Korea's COVID response (testing/tracing) was praised as a global model

2025.event: South Korea leads in semiconductor manufacturing and AI development
